response: The global food system faces mounting pressure as the population is expected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring 60% more food production. This challenge is intensified by climate change, water scarcity, and soil degradation, while agriculture itself generates about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions and 33% of the world’s arable land has already lost productivity. The text argues that food waste—about one-third of all food produced—and unsustainable farming practices make the crisis worse. It highlights potential solutions such as AI-driven precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, regenerative farming, and policy reforms, but stresses that scaling these affordably for the poorest populations is the central challenge.
